title: Aporum teloense
taxonomy: Aporum teloense is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Aporum. The genus Aporum is part of the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. Orchidaceae is further grouped into the order Asparagales, a clade of monocot flowering plants. Asparagales is part of the clade Angiosperm, which includes all flowering plants. This comprehensive classification highlights the evolutionary relationships among Aporum teloense and other flowering plant species.

intro: Aporum teloense, also known as the Telo's Dendrobium, is a type of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aporum teloense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aporum teloense is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and tinctures.
